overview.summary: Vectors are arrays that grow automatically when they run out of space.
overview.interviewRelevance: Python lists and JS arrays are vectors. Using them effectively is crucial.

paragraphs: Python lists grow dynamically because they are vectors under the hood.
When you append to a list and it runs out of space, Python allocates a new buffer 1.125x larger and copies everything over.
This mechanism is hidden, but understanding it is essential for reasoning about performance in tight loops.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: This is why append is O(1) amortized — most appends are free, occasional copies are shared across many operations.

definition.technicalDefinition: A dynamic array (vector) is a resizable array that manages its own memory buffer, allocating a larger buffer and copying elements when capacity is exhausted.

vocabulary.definition: The number of items currently stored in the vector.
The maximum number of items the current memory buffer can hold before it needs to resize.
The average time per operation evaluated over a sequence of operations.

keyProperties: Appending to the end is O(1) amortized time.
Inserting in the middle is O(n) because subsequent elements must shift.

operations.name: Append (Push Back)
operations.description: Add an element to the end of the array. If capacity is reached, allocate a new wider array and copy.
operations.visualNote: Element slots into the next free index. On resize, the whole array jumps.

operations.name: Pop from End
operations.description: Remove the last element and decrease length.
operations.visualNote: The length counter shrinks. The memory capacity is unchanged.

operations.name: Insert/Delete Middle
operations.description: Insert or delete at a specific index, shifting all later elements.
operations.visualNote: All subsequent elements move right or left to accommodate the change.

practice.title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1)
practice.keyInsight: Combine a vector for O(1) random access with a hash map.

practice.title: Design Browser History
practice.keyInsight: Vectors simulate the back/forward history array.

practice.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
practice.keyInsight: In-place modifications of a fixed vector.
